features.details: Review the existing sidebar component code to understand its structure, CSS classes, and how it integrates with the overall admin layout. Identify the files and components that need modification.

features.description: Add responsive CSS media queries for sidebar
features.details: Implement CSS media queries to detect mobile screen sizes and apply appropriate styles for the sidebar. Set breakpoints for mobile devices (typically < 768px) and define collapsed/expanded states.
Add a hamburger menu or toggle button that appears on mobile devices to control the sidebar expand/collapse state. The button should be hidden on desktop view.
Add JavaScript logic to manage the sidebar's open/closed state. Handle click events on the toggle button, update CSS classes, and optionally persist state in localStorage for user preference.
Implement CSS transitions for smooth expand/collapse animations. Ensure animations are performant on mobile devices using transform and opacity properties.

features.description: Add overlay backdrop for mobile sidebar
features.details: When the sidebar is expanded on mobile, add a semi-transparent overlay backdrop that closes the sidebar when clicked. This improves UX by providing a clear way to dismiss the sidebar.
Ensure touch interactions work properly on mobile devices. Consider adding swipe gestures to open/close the sidebar for better mobile UX.

Test the sidebar functionality on various screen sizes and devices. Verify that desktop view remains unchanged and mobile view works correctly. Test on Chrome DevTools mobile emulation and real devices.

features.description: Add unit tests for sidebar functionality
features.details: Write unit tests to verify sidebar toggle functionality, state management, and responsive behavior. Test edge cases like rapid clicking and state persistence.
Document the new mobile sidebar feature, including usage instructions and any configuration options for developers.

overall_approach: Implement a responsive sidebar that automatically collapses on mobile devices with a toggle button to expand/collapse. This involves CSS media queries for responsive behavior, a toggle button component, state management for sidebar visibility, and smooth transition animations for better UX.
potential_risks: Existing desktop layout may be affected by responsive changes
State management for sidebar toggle may conflict with other UI state
Animation performance on lower-end mobile devices
Z-index conflicts with other overlay elements
Touch interaction issues on mobile devices
